99dresses.Name: 99dresses
99dresses.What They Did: An online fashion platform allowing users to trade dresses and fashion items.
99dresses.Why They Failed: High customer acquisition costs, low retention, and failure of a crucial funding round.
99dresses.Takeaway: Balance growth with unit economics and secure backup funding.

AllRomance.Name: AllRomance
AllRomance.What They Did: E-book retailer specializing in romance novels.
AllRomance.Why They Failed: Slow adaptation to Kindle/Amazon ecosystem and unsustainable royalty payouts leading to severe financial losses.
AllRomance.Takeaway: Adapt quickly to platform changes and control margins.

Ahalife.Name: Ahalife
Ahalife.What They Did: Curated luxury goods and fashion marketplace.
Ahalife.Why They Failed: High marketing costs to acquire luxury buyers and inability to defend its niche against Amazon.
Ahalife.Takeaway: Luxury niches require high organic retention to sustain high acquisition costs.

Airy Labs.Name: Airy Labs
Airy Labs.What They Did: Educational mobile games and apps for children.
Airy Labs.Why They Failed: Rapid cash burn during a chaotic product sprint, launching too many games without focus or product-market fit.
Airy Labs.Takeaway: Focus on a single high-quality core product before scaling.

Codeacademy.Name: Codeacademy
Codeacademy.What They Did: Online interactive coding lessons and technical education platform.
Codeacademy.Why They Failed: Acquired in 2021 and closed in 2022 due to failure of free-tier users to convert to paid subscriptions and intense competition from Coursera.
Codeacademy.Takeaway: Freemium models must have clear paths to monetization.

Edmodo.Name: Edmodo
Edmodo.What They Did: Social learning and educational platform for K-12 students and teachers.
Edmodo.Why They Failed: Monetization struggles and inability to compete with Google Classroom and Zoom during the remote learning pivot.
Edmodo.Takeaway: Platforms need a monetization strategy that doesn't rely purely on ad revenue or school budgets.

Brex.Name: Brex
Brex.What They Did: Corporate credit cards and cash management for startups.
Brex.Why They Failed: Included in benchmark for comparison. High cash burn rate and credit risk from early-stage startups.
Brex.Takeaway: Credit risk and underwriting standards must be robust when serving high-risk startup clients.

Clinkle.Name: Clinkle
Clinkle.Why They Failed: Massive overhype and failure to launch a viable product before being beaten by Apple Pay and PayPal.
Clinkle.Takeaway: Build a working product and validate with users before spending heavily on marketing.

Cardless.Name: Cardless
Cardless.What They Did: Credit cards and co-branded card programs for brands and startups.
Cardless.Why They Failed: Included in benchmark for comparison. High cost of customer acquisition and complexity of managing bank partnership compliance.
Cardless.Takeaway: Fintechs must navigate regulatory compliance and bank partnerships carefully to scale.
